news.title: Nayanthara buys duplex apartment in Chennai's Poes Garden for ₹31.5 crore
news.para: South Indian star Nayanthara and husband Vignesh Shivan have purchased a luxurious duplex in Chennai's exclusive Poes Garden for Rs 31.5 crore. The sprawling 14,369 sq ft property, located in the Legacy project, underscores the enclave's status as a prime address for celebrities and high-net-worth individuals, with property values consistently reaching crores.

news.title: Ludhiana development body issues notices to two builders over unauthorized constructions
news.para: Glada has initiated a zero-tolerance policy against unauthorized construction, issuing notices for deviations from approved building plans. This crackdown includes actions against commercial activity beyond sanctioned limits and significant structural violations. Residents are warned that non-compliance will lead to plan cancellation, sealing, or demolition, with full responsibility falling on owners.

news.title: Pay 11% interest from March 2021 till flat handover: Haryana RERA to Burman Estate
news.para: Haryana Rera has ordered Burman Estate Pvt Ltd to pay delay possession charges to two allottees for their serviced apartment in Gurgaon Spectrum Centre. The developer failed to offer possession by the stipulated March 15, 2021 deadline, despite the Occupation Certificate being issued. Rera rejected the developer's plea for a second Covid-19 related extension.

news.title: Administration reclaims Nazul land in Kanpur after illegal sale
news.para: Kanpur District Magistrate has ordered government re-entry into a valuable Nazul property in Civil Lines, valued at Rs 24.77 crore. The land was illegally sold to a private builder in 2012 by heirs who concealed facts about the lease. The sale occurred despite non-payment of lease rent and lease renewal, violating Nazul Manual provisions.

news.title: Telangana RERA allows homebuyers to finish stalled project
news.para: Telangana authorities have empowered homebuyers to complete stalled housing projects after a builder's default, a first under the RERA Act. Flat owners formed associations to finish construction on two projects by Jayathri Infrastructures. This move safeguards investments and ensures project completion, with one project already finished and another underway.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh: Water bodies must in real estate projects spanning 10 acres
news.para: New regulations mandate that real estate projects over 10 acres must allocate 1% of land for waterbodies to recharge groundwater and recycle water for non-potable uses. This initiative aims to combat urban runoff and the 'urban island effect'. Additionally, developers must minimize concrete in green spaces and use permeable materials for jogging tracks and walkways, ensuring water seepage.

news.title: Maharashtra CM announces ₹23,487-crore metro line to Gateway of India, underground tunnel network
news.para: Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis unveiled ambitious infrastructure plans. The Budget 2026-27 includes extensive underground tunnel networks in Mumbai and Pune. A Rs 23,487 crore metro line to the Gateway of India is also planned. The state aims for 1,200 km of metro lines and over 6,000 km of expressways by 2047, enhancing connectivity for passengers and freight.

news.title: House prices in UK rise but inflation fears cloud outlook
news.para: In February, UK house prices experienced their quickest surge since October, signaling a vibrant market. Yet, rising mortgage rates are casting a shadow, fueled by inflationary concerns linked to tensions in the Middle East. Consequently, financial markets anticipate a reduced likelihood of interest rate reductions from the Bank of England, placing additional strain on housing affordability for prospective buyers.

news.title: Non-tribals barred from purchasing land in Meghalaya's Garo Hills region
news.para: The Garo Hills Autonomous District Council has prohibited non-tribal individuals from purchasing or claiming land in Garo Hills districts. This move aims to safeguard tribal land rights and traditional ownership. Existing legal landholdings of non-tribals remain unaffected. The directive is effective immediately, reinforcing indigenous community protections.

news.title: Karnataka CM says government pursuing 'balanced' development strategy in Budget 2026
news.para: The budget, totaling Rs 4,48,004 crore, focuses on a development plan that mixes welfare with infrastructure and long-term economic changes. Key proposals include an IT Park in Mangaluru, making Mysuru Karnataka's second IT hub, and creating a Bangalore Robotics and AI Innovation Zone.

news.title: Maharashtra budget outlines urban expansion, metro network target of 1,200 km
news.para: In the Maharashtra State Budget 2026-27, Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis on Friday outlined a major shift in urban governance, projecting that 70 per cent of the state's population will live in urban areas by 2047 and contribute nearly 80 per cent of Maharashtra's GDP.

news.title: Government's primary objective is to make Bengaluru 'world's most liveable city': Karnataka CM
news.para: Karnataka Chief Minister Siddaramaiah unveiled plans to make Bengaluru the "world's most liveable city" by mobilising resources through Municipal Bonds and a Rs 7,000 crore grant. The budget includes significant investments in road development, white topping, footpaths, and the implementation of a Revised Master Plan-2041 and Comprehensive Mobility Plan.

news.title: Amitabh Bachchan acquires 2.67 acre land parcel for ₹35-crore in Ayodhya
news.para: Bollywood megastar Amitabh Bachchan has further expanded his Ayodhya real estate portfolio with a significant 2.67-acre land acquisition for ₹35 crore. This marks his third investment in the holy city through AB Corp, following a previous purchase in The Sarayu project.

news.title: ETRECA 2026 honours industry leaders across residential, commercial and PropTech segments
news.para: The Economic Times Real Estate Conclave & Awards 2026 convened industry leaders to discuss India's property sector's evolving dynamics, focusing on affordability, redevelopment, and future demand. The event also celebrated excellence across residential, commercial, and PropTech segments, recognizing key players for their contributions and innovations.

news.title: SAMHI Hotels to invest ₹470 million for 70% stake in RARE India
news.para: SAMHI Hotels is acquiring a 70% stake in RARE India for approximately ₹470 million, marking its entry into the experiential leisure hospitality segment. This investment will bolster RARE India's capabilities and reach. Additionally, SAMHI and RARE India are exploring an affiliation with Marriott International to operate RARE's portfolio under Marriott's Outdoor Collection brand.

news.title: ‘No apparent error’: Allahabad HC rejects GNIDA’s review on IT firm plot
news.para: The Allahabad High Court has rejected a review petition by Greater Noida Industrial Development Authority. The court previously overturned the cancellation of a 25-acre IT plot allotted to Elevator Properties. The Authority failed to follow a statutory deadline for issuing notices to the developer. This ruling upholds the original allotment, emphasizing procedural lapses by the Authority.

news.title: Secunderabad cantonment board announces OTS for property tax arrears
news.para: Secunderabad Cantonment Board (SCB) has launched a one-time settlement scheme for property tax arrears, offering a 30% rebate on accumulated interest or ₹10,000, whichever is higher. Property owners must pay their full outstanding dues by March 31 to avail this concession, aiming to encourage voluntary settlement and reduce long-standing liabilities.

news.title: Haryana plans to mandate registration of property sale agreement
news.para: Haryana plans to mandate registration of Agreements to Sell to enhance transparency and legal security in property deals. This move aims to curb fraud and litigation by ensuring all property commitments are formally documented, unlike current practices of using plain paper or notarized agreements with weaker legal standing.
